{{Quote|I've seen Imperial cruisers destroyed in the Nebulas of Osssorck; fought my way through the dust storms of Voniss; stared down Dark Grahn's mercenary army; now, all I can stare down is the bottom of a bottle of cheap wine, kid.|A gunfighter|<ref name="AFoC">{{WEGCite|book=Star Wars Gamemaster Screen|story=A Fistful of Credits}}</ref>}}
The '''Nebulas of Osssorck''',<ref name="AFoC" /> also known as the '''Osssorck Nebulae''', were a series of [[Nebula/Legends|nebulosities]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], an aging yet legendary [[Unidentified gunfighter|gunfighter]] resided in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Buerhoz]] on the<ref name="AFoC" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="EAOC Clakdor">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Colu system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]]. When a group of individuals encountered the gunfighter at Buerhoz, he claimed to have witnessed the destruction of several [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the Nebulas of Osssorck.<ref name="AFoC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Nebulas of Osssorck were introduced via mention in "[[A Fistful of Credits]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure written by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] and included in the [[1992]] supplement ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AFoC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' provided an alternate name for the nebulae and placed them in grid square J-12.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
